content: All five plan steps' stated success conditions verified against a fresh re-read of the three edited files (not the Maker-reported diffs): trail-schema.md has the 00-deps.json layout entry and full section; orchestrator-role/SKILL.md step 1 has the appended instruction; checker-role/SKILL.md has 5 numbered checks in the correct order; earned-constraints.md has EC-010 appended with prior content (EC-009, EC-VERIFY-FIRST-001, format/seed sections) byte-identical to the pre-edit read.

This cycle did not touch catalog/skills.json or either marketplace.json twin -- confirmed by re-reading Make's own log (seq 5) and by the fact none of the four edited files are schema-governed artifacts. Not applicable, not a pass-by-default.

This trail's own 00-deps.json does not exist -- this cycle was dispatched with the standard fixed role set (planner/maker/checker/reflector) and no dependency-resolver invocation was made explicit in this session. Since the new check-3 requirement lands in checker-role/SKILL.md as part of THIS cycle's own Make output, it cannot retroactively apply to this same cycle's step 1, which already ran before the edit existed. Not scored as a failure; flagged for Reflector as a self-referential edge case worth a note in the trail-schema or orchestrator-role text (does the new requirement apply starting next cycle, or should orchestrator write 00-deps.json even for a cycle whose own Make is the change being applied?).

Re-read .agents/rules/ Laws 1-4 against what Make actually did: no domain SKILL.md was touched (orchestrator/checker are Platform role-skills, not one of the 10 C-Suite domains, so Law 4 -- domain boundaries -- is not implicated). No declarative-params or verification-placeholder violations found in the four diffs.

The TYPE1 write this cycle performs (editing live Platform files under catalog/) was explicitly authorized by the human via /ceo:approve-initiative, who selected scope 'Both (dependency-resolver hardening + EC-010)' against trail e5e3107f-17d3-4260-abca-06b3efec5c57 before this cycle's Make step ran. This IS the sanctioned HIL checkpoint hil-gating.md requires -- not a self-seal bypassing it.
